Description: Performs no operation. Used for padding and timing.
Copies the contents of source register rs to destination register rd.
Zero-extends a 15-bit immediate value and performs bitwise OR with rs; storing the result in rd.
Loads a 15-bit immediate value into bits 49-63 of rd; effectively loading the upper portion of a 64-bit register when used with rs.
Adds a sign-extended 15-bit immediate value to the contents of rs and stores the result in rd.

Operation: rd = rs1; if rs1 != 0 then atomic_inc(mem[rs1 - 16]:64)
Description: Increments the managed-object reference count header without trapping and returns the original object pointer.
Decrements the managed-object reference count header without trapping; rd is 1 when the object reaches zero and must be released by runtime slow path.
Invalidates instruction/JIT cache state for the byte range [base base+size).
Adds the contents of rs1 and rs2 and stores the 64-bit result in rd.
Subtracts the contents of rs2 from rs1 and stores the 64-bit result in rd.
Multiplies the contents of rs1 and rs2 and stores the lower 64 bits of the result in rd.
Performs signed integer division of rs1 by rs2 and stores the quotient in rd.
Performs unsigned integer division of rs1 by rs2 and stores the quotient in rd.
Performs signed integer division of rs1 by rs2 and stores the remainder in rd.
Shifts the contents of rs1 left by the number of bits specified in rs2 (mod 64) and stores the result in rd.
Performs logical right shift of rs1 by the number of bits specified in rs2 (mod 64) and stores the result in rd.
Performs arithmetic right shift of rs1 by the number of bits specified in rs2 (mod 64) and stores the result in rd.
Performs bitwise AND of rs1 and rs2 and stores the result in rd.
Performs bitwise OR of rs1 and rs2 and stores the result in rd.
Performs bitwise XOR of rs1 and rs2 and stores the result in rd.
Performs bitwise NOT (one's complement) of rs and stores the result in rd.
Compares rs1 and rs2 for equality. Stores 1 in rd if equal; 0 otherwise.
Compares rs1 and rs2 for inequality. Stores 1 in rd if not equal; 0 otherwise.
Compares rs1 and rs2 (signed). Stores 1 in rd if rs1 < rs2; 0 otherwise.
Compares rs1 and rs2 (signed). Stores 1 in rd if rs1 <= rs2; 0 otherwise.
Compares floating-point values in rs1 and rs2 for equality. Stores 1 in rd if equal; 0 otherwise.
Compares floating-point values in rs1 and rs2. Stores 1 in rd if rs1 < rs2; 0 otherwise.
Compares floating-point values in rs1 and rs2. Stores 1 in rd if rs1 <= rs2; 0 otherwise.
Adds floating-point values in rs1 and rs2 and stores the result in rd.
Subtracts floating-point value in rs2 from rs1 and stores the result in rd.
Multiplies floating-point values in rs1 and rs2 and stores the result in rd.
Divides floating-point value in rs1 by rs2 and stores the result in rd.

Operation: if rs1 == rs2 then pc += sign_extend(imm15) * 4
Description: Branches to PC + sign_extend(imm15) * 4 if rs1 equals rs2.

Operation: if rs1 != rs2 then pc += sign_extend(imm15) * 4
Description: Branches to PC + sign_extend(imm15) * 4 if rs1 does not equal rs2.

Operation: if rs1 < rs2 (signed) then pc += sign_extend(imm15) * 4
Description: Branches to PC + sign_extend(imm15) * 4 if rs1 is less than rs2 (signed).

Operation: if rs1 <= rs2 (signed) then pc += sign_extend(imm15) * 4
Description: Branches to PC + sign_extend(imm15) * 4 if rs1 is less than or equal to rs2 (signed).
Performs an unconditional jump to PC + sign_extend(offset) * 4.
Jumps to PC + sign_extend(offset) * 4 and stores the return address (PC+4) in rd (typically r29).
Jumps to rs + sign_extend(imm15) and stores the return address (PC+4) in rd (typically r29).
Returns from a subroutine by jumping to the address stored in the link register (r29).
Loads a signed byte from memory at [addr + imm15] and sign-extends it to 64 bits in rd.
Loads an unsigned byte from memory at [addr + imm15] and zero-extends it to 64 bits in rd.
Loads a signed half-word (16 bits) from memory at [addr + imm15] and sign-extends it to 64 bits in rd.
Loads an unsigned half-word (16 bits) from memory at [addr + imm15] and zero-extends it to 64 bits in rd.
Loads a signed word (32 bits) from memory at [addr + imm15] and sign-extends it to 64 bits in rd.
Loads an unsigned word (32 bits) from memory at [addr + imm15] and zero-extends it to 64 bits in rd.
Loads a double-word (64 bits) from memory at [addr + imm15] into rd.
Loads two adjacent 64-bit words from memory. Precise exception order matches two LD.D operations.
Stores the lower 8 bits of register data to memory at [addr + imm15].
Stores the lower 16 bits of register data to memory at [addr + imm15].
Stores the lower 32 bits of register data to memory at [addr + imm15].
Stores the 64-bit register data to memory at [addr + imm15].
Stores two adjacent 64-bit words to memory. Precise exception order matches two ST.D operations.
Calculates the address [addr + sign_extend(imm15)] and stores it in rd. Used for pointer arithmetic.
Decrements the stack pointer (r31) by 8 and stores register rs at the top of the stack.
Loads the value from the top of the stack into rd and increments the stack pointer (r31) by 8.

Operation: sp -= 16; mem[sp] = r29; mem[sp+8] = r30; r30 = sp; sp -= sign_extend(imm15)
Description: Establishes a new stack frame: saves r29 and r30; sets r30 to current sp; and decrements sp by imm15.

Operation: sp = r30; r29 = mem[sp]; r30 = mem[sp+8]; sp += 16
Description: Tears down the stack frame: restores r29 and r30; and increments sp to the old value.
Adjusts the stack pointer (r31) by a sign-extended 15-bit immediate value.
Calculates the address [r30 + sign_extend(imm15)] and stores it in rd. Used for local variable access.
Jumps to PC + sign_extend(offset) * 4 and saves return address in rd (r29). Used for direct calls.
Performs an unconditional tail-call jump to PC + sign_extend(offset) * 4 without saving return address.
Triggers a system call to the operating system using the ID in imm15. Arguments passed in r2 (and r3 for two-arg syscalls). Result stored in rd.
Triggers a software breakpoint trap to the host environment or debugger.
Traps to supervisor mode. Sets sepc=PC scause=8 and jumps to stvec.BASE.
Returns from supervisor trap. Restores privilege from sstatus.SPP and jumps to sepc.
Loads a doubleword from memory at rs1 and creates a reservation for a subsequent SC.D.
Writes rs2 to memory at rs1 if reservation is still valid. rd=0 on success; nonzero on failure.
Atomically reads CSR at address imm15 into rd and writes rs to it. If rs=r0 the write is suppressed.
Synchronizes page-table updates with address translation (TLB flush). All prior stores to page tables are visible to subsequent translations.
Configures an optional hardware loop counter and backward branch displacement.

Operation: loop_count--; if loop_count != 0 then pc += sign_extend(imm15) * 4
Description: Decrements the active hardware loop counter and branches while nonzero. Interpreter/JIT may lower to a counted branch.
Advisory read prefetch. Must not raise visible faults and may be implemented as a no-op.
Advisory write-intent prefetch. Must not raise visible faults and may be implemented as a no-op.
Advisory non-temporal prefetch for streaming data. Must not raise visible faults and may be implemented as a no-op.
Advisory hint that a byte range will be zeroed. Correct software must still perform stores or call a runtime helper.
Optional thread-local allocation-buffer fast path. A zero result means software must call the runtime allocator.
Reads an HVM profiling/performance counter subject to privilege and platform policy.
Checks tagged or bounded pointer metadata and traps on violation when HVM-Cap is enabled.

Operation: if addr == 0 then trap_null else rd = mem[addr + imm15]:64
Description: Loads a 64-bit value and folds a null-check trap into the load when HVM-NZ is enabled.
Advisory branch/code-layout hint. It must not affect architectural correctness and may be a no-op.
Signals an accelerator or GPU command queue through a memory-mapped doorbell when HVM-A is enabled.
Configures HVM-V vector length and element type.
Loads active vector elements from contiguous memory.
Stores active vector elements to contiguous memory.
Loads active vector elements using a byte stride.
Stores active vector elements using a byte stride.
Loads active vector elements using vector indexes.
Stores active vector elements using vector indexes.
Adds active vector elements.
Adds scalar rs2 to active vector elements.
Subtracts active vector elements.
Subtracts scalar rs2 from active vector elements.
Multiplies active vector elements.
Multiplies active vector elements by scalar rs2.
Divides active vector elements.
Divides active vector elements by scalar rs2.
Floating-point fused multiply-accumulate on active vector elements.
Floating-point scalar-vector fused multiply-accumulate on active vector elements.
Compares active vector elements and writes a mask vector.
Compares active vector elements against scalar rs2 and writes a mask vector.
Merges active vector elements using a mask.
Returns the first active set bit index in a vector mask or -1 if none.
Reduces active vector elements by addition into scalar element vd[0].
Reduces active vector elements by minimum into scalar element vd[0].
Reduces active vector elements by maximum into scalar element vd[0].
Shifts active vector elements left by vector counts.
Shifts active vector elements left by scalar count.
Logically shifts active vector elements right by vector counts.
Logically shifts active vector elements right by scalar count.
Bitwise AND of active vector elements.
Bitwise OR of active vector elements.
Bitwise XOR of active vector elements.
